error_signature: Access to the file is not allowed. Allowed paths: ...
title: File access not allowed due to restrictions
symptom: When trying to read or write a file on a network share, n8n throws 'Access to the file is not allowed'. This occurs despite having the correct permissions set on the folder.
applies_when: Accessing files on a network share
not_this_if: Using an older version of n8n without this issue
root_cause: The error occurs because the N8N_RESTRICT_FILE_ACCESS_TO environment variable is not correctly configured. In version 2.10.4, this variable requires a semicolon-separated list of allowed paths, and incorrect formatting can lead to access issues.
fix_steps: Open the n8n environment configuration file.
Ensure the N8N_RESTRICT_FILE_ACCESS_TO variable is set with semicolon-separated paths.
Restart n8n to apply the changes.
agent_instruction: Correct the N8N_RESTRICT_FILE_ACCESS_TO variable and restart n8n.
